text:
Market today: Mood is cautious-to-nervous. The dominant driver is fear that the chip/tech bull run is cracking — exactly your world. Margin amplifies every dip, so the risk here is real and personal.

Your holdings: PLTR is the loudest alarm — down 13% and scanner says SELL; this one needs a hard look now. AVGO is your largest position, down nearly 13%, and scanner says TRIM — on margin, a big losing position in a wobbly chip tape is a double threat. PTNM is a tiny spec down 15% with a stop only $0.89 away — it's on the razor's edge. SPCX is a brand-new, very volatile IPO with limited data; manage it manually and keep size small. Margin amplifies every one of these drops.

Watch: (1) Whether chip/semiconductor sentiment deteriorates further — SMH is your early warning signal. (2) AVGO holding above its $336 stop — if that breaks, your biggest position becomes your biggest problem.

Bottom line: Your margin book is bleeding in exactly the sectors the market is most worried about — PLTR and AVGO need decisions today, not tomorrow.
